title: First and Emergency Aid Program Learning Outcomes
content:
First and Emergency Aid Program Learning Outcomes

Program Learning Outcomes

1. Possesses fundamental, current, and practical knowledge related to the profession.

2. Has knowledge about occupational health and safety, environmental awareness, and quality processes.

3. Follows and effectively uses current developments and practices related to the profession.

4. Effectively uses information technologies relevant to the profession (software, programs, animations, etc.).

5. Has the ability to independently assess and propose solutions to professional problems and issues using analytical and critical thinking.

6. Can effectively express thoughts at the level of their knowledge and skills in written and oral communication.

7. Takes responsibility as a team member to solve complex and unforeseen problems encountered in field-related practices.

8. Has awareness of career management and lifelong learning.

9. Respects social, scientific, cultural, and ethical values during the collection, implementation, and dissemination of field-related data.

10. Follows field-related information and communicates with colleagues using the Turkish language and a foreign language.

11. Explains and applies basic and advanced life support.

12. Identifies and uses the equipment utilized in the delivery of emergency health services.

13. Ensures and manages the safety of self, the patient, and the scene during the delivery of emergency health services.

14. Explains the legal regulations related to emergency health services.
